headline: Twenty-nine keywords, mostly brand.
standfirst: From the Semrush Organic Rankings export for the AE database on June 3, 2026. The keyword count dropped from ~52 in January to 29 today, then ticked back up 3.57% this week. Of those 29, the brand term 'cms prime' alone drives 89.7% of all organic traffic — the recovery target is moving demand toward non-brand commercial queries.

feature_chart.title: Tracked organic keywords, six months · AE database
feature_chart.subtitle: Total keywords cmsprime.com ranks for in UAE Google
feature_chart.caption: The drop from ~52 in January to ~33 by March looks like a Google algorithm reweighting — keywords dropped out of the top 100 in February and never came back. The flat 29-30 plateau since April reflects the brand-dominated mix: those keywords are stable but few. The +3.57% uptick this week, while small in absolute terms, is the first weekly increase since the February reset.

country_footprint.title: Where cmsprime.com ranks, by database
country_footprint.subtitle: Same domain, three Google jurisdictions, very different keyword footprints

top_keywords.title: The five keywords driving organic traffic
top_keywords.subtitle: Position, volume, and share of total organic traffic
top_keywords.footnote: Top 5 of 29 tracked keywords accounts for 99.04% of organic traffic. Heavy reliance on a single brand term — long-tail commercial queries are the unmet opportunity.

intent_split.title: Keywords by search intent
intent_split.subtitle: What people actually want when they search these terms
intent_split.footnote: Informational queries dominate keyword count but drive almost no traffic — that's typical when a site ranks for the FAQ-style long tail but not at the top. Navigational (brand) is small in count but supplies 97% of traffic.

top_pages.title: Top pages by organic traffic share
top_pages.subtitle: Five pages account for the entire indexable footprint
top_pages.footnote: The homepage alone soaks up 97.19% of organic traffic. That's a single-point-of-failure pattern — when the homepage's ranking moves, everything moves.

competitors.title: Main organic competitors in AE
competitors.subtitle: Domains overlapping keywords with cmsprime.com
competitors.footnote: 131 competitors tracked overall. The top one (cmsfinancial.ae) is a brand-name lookalike — much of the overlap is people typing the wrong CMS.

position_changes.title: Position changes this period
position_changes.subtitle: New, lost, improved, declined
position_changes.detail: No keywords entered or exited the top 100. No position shifts of 3+ places. This is the pre-deployment baseline — the hreflang and Organization schema work landed June 1, and the position-tracker reindex window is 14-28 days. Re-measurement expected July 1.

prose.title: Reading the rankings, honestly.
paragraphs: Twenty-nine ranking keywords sounds small. It is small. But the relevant question isn't the count — it's the composition. Of those 29, only 2 are transactional. The other 27 split between informational (FAQ-style) and navigational (brand). When people search 'cms prime' or 'cms prime dubai', they find us. When they search 'forex broker dubai' or 'cfd trading platform', they don't.

The drop from 52 to 29 keywords over six months is concerning on the surface — but it's mostly noise from long-tail keywords cycling in and out of the top 100. The 27-keyword commercial recovery list documented in the Keywords chapter is where ranking ground was actually lost.

The brand-versus-commercial gap is the real story. 96.7% of organic traffic comes through 'cms prime' branded searches. The 3.3% that isn't brand-driven is what the technical fixes (FAQ schema, hreflang, enriched Organization markup) deployed June 1 are meant to expand. Expect the first signs on the July 1 reading.

pull_quote.text: When people search 'cms prime', they find us. When they search 'forex broker dubai', they don't.
